nameMeanings.name: Gresham
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Yorkshire and Lincolnshire): habitational name from a place in Norfolk, so named from Old English græs, gærs ‘grass(land), pasturage’ + hām ‘homestead’ or hamm ‘enclosure hemmed in by water’.

variants.name: Grisham
meanings: English: variant of   Gresham  . This form of the surname is now rare in Britain.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: When William Gresham was born in 1483, in Holt, Norfolk, England, his father, John Gresham, was 28 and his mother, Lady Alice Blythe, was 18. He died on 28 March 1548, in London St Mary le Bow and St Pancras Soper Lane with All Hallows Honey Lane, Middlesex, England, at the age of 65.

ko.lifeSummary: William Gresham(이)가 1483년 영국 노르폴크 홀트에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Gresham(은)는 28세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Alice Blythe(은)는 18세였다. 그는 1548년 3월 28일 영국 미들레세스 런던 스트 메리 일 보 앤드 스트 파크라스 소퍼 랜 위스 앨 핼로스 호니 랜에서 65세의 나이로 사망했다.
